question: What is the number one cause of early skin ageing?
options: Eating too much sugar
Not using enough moisturiser
Sun exposure without protection which causes up to 80 percent of visible skin ageing
Only genetics determines how fast you age

question: What is collagen and why does it matter for skin?
options: A type of skincare product applied topically
A protein that gives skin its structure firmness and plumpness and decreases naturally with age
A vitamin found in anti-ageing creams
A natural oil produced by skin to keep it hydrated

question: At what age does collagen production start to decline?
options: At age 60
At age 50
From around age 25 collagen production begins to slowly decrease each year
Collagen production never declines naturally

question: What are fine lines?
options: Deep creases that form in older age
Early shallow lines that appear on skin often caused by repetitive facial movements and decreasing collagen
Lines on the palms of hands
Stretch marks on the face

question: What are wrinkles?
options: A type of acne scar
Deeper more established lines and folds in skin caused by aging sun damage loss of elasticity and collagen
Temporary marks left by sleeping on your face
Dark spots caused by sun exposure

question: What is elastin and what does it do for skin?
options: A type of moisturiser for mature skin
A protein that works alongside collagen giving skin the ability to stretch and snap back into place
A vitamin that brightens aging skin
A natural SPF produced by skin

question: What skincare ingredient is considered the gold standard for anti-ageing?
options: Hyaluronic acid
Vitamin C
Retinol which stimulates collagen production and speeds up cell turnover
Niacinamide

question: What does retinol do for ageing skin?
options: Only moisturises dry mature skin
Stimulates collagen production speeds up cell turnover and reduces the appearance of fine lines wrinkles and uneven tone
Provides SPF protection for mature skin
Only brightens dark spots without any anti-ageing benefits

question: What is photoageing?
options: Skin ageing caused purely by genetics
Premature skin ageing caused specifically by UV radiation from the sun including wrinkles dark spots and loss of elasticity
Skin ageing caused by stress and poor sleep
A type of ageing only affecting fair-skinned people

question: What are crow's feet?
options: Dark circles under the eyes
Fine lines and wrinkles that fan out from the outer corners of the eyes caused by facial expressions and ageing
Puffiness under the eyes
Age spots on the cheeks

question: What is the best anti-ageing product you can use every single day?
options: An expensive retinol serum
A vitamin C serum
A broad spectrum SPF sunscreen which prevents further UV damage that causes ageing
A collagen-boosting supplement

question: What does glycation do to skin and why is it an ageing concern?
options: Glycation adds a healthy glow to mature skin
Excess sugar in the diet attaches to collagen fibres making them stiff and prone to breaking down which accelerates skin ageing
Glycation is a process that boosts skin hydration
Glycation only affects body skin not the face

question: What is the difference between intrinsic and extrinsic ageing?
options: They are exactly the same process
Intrinsic ageing is natural ageing from genetics and time while extrinsic ageing is caused by external factors like sun stress pollution and lifestyle
Intrinsic ageing only affects the body and extrinsic only affects the face
Intrinsic ageing can be stopped with skincare but extrinsic cannot

question: What lifestyle habit accelerates skin ageing more than almost anything else besides sun exposure?
options: Drinking herbal tea
Eating fruit
Smoking which reduces blood flow breaks down collagen and causes premature wrinkles and dullness
Getting regular exercise

question: What does peptide do in an anti-ageing skincare product?
options: Physically fills in wrinkles like filler
Sends signals to skin cells to produce more collagen and elastin helping to firm and smooth skin over time
Provides instant visible lifting and tightening
Only hydrates skin without any anti-ageing benefit

question: What causes age spots or liver spots on skin?
options: Poor diet and lack of vitamins
Accumulated sun damage over years causing clusters of melanin to appear as flat dark spots on skin
A sign of liver disease appearing on the skin
Normal natural ageing with no UV involvement

question: What ingredient helps fade age spots and hyperpigmentation from ageing?
options: Retinol only targets wrinkles not spots
Vitamin C niacinamide and alpha arbutin which brighten and even out skin tone
Heavy occlusant creams
Physical exfoliants like sugar scrubs

question: What is the décolletage and why does it need anti-ageing attention?
options: The forehead area prone to deep wrinkles
The chest and neck area which is often exposed to sun and shows ageing early with wrinkles and sun damage
The area around the eyes
The cheeks which lose volume first with age

question: What does free radical damage do to skin over time?
options: Free radicals improve skin elasticity
Free radicals are unstable molecules from UV pollution and stress that damage skin cells break down collagen and accelerate ageing
Free radicals only affect oily skin
Free radicals help skin produce more collagen

question: What are antioxidants and why are they important in anti-ageing skincare?
options: Antioxidants are exfoliants that remove dead skin
Antioxidants neutralise free radicals preventing the cellular damage that accelerates skin ageing
Antioxidants are moisturising ingredients for dry skin
Antioxidants are only found in food supplements not skincare

question: Which antioxidant is one of the most popular in anti-ageing serums?
options: Glycolic acid
Salicylic acid
Vitamin C which protects against free radicals and also brightens and boosts collagen
Hyaluronic acid

question: What happens to skin volume as we age?
options: Skin gains volume evenly across the face with age
Fat pads in the face shrink and shift causing hollowing sagging and a loss of the youthful plump appearance
Skin volume only changes in the eye area
Volume loss only happens after age 70

question: What is a nasolabial fold?
options: A type of wrinkle that forms on the forehead
The deep lines that run from the sides of the nose down to the corners of the mouth becoming more prominent with age
A term for wrinkles around the eyes
Dark circles under the eyes

question: What does hyaluronic acid do for ageing skin?
options: It boosts collagen production like retinol
It deeply hydrates skin by attracting water which temporarily plumps skin and reduces the appearance of fine lines
It removes age spots and hyperpigmentation
It exfoliates ageing skin to reveal a brighter complexion

question: What is bakuchiol and why has it become popular in anti-ageing skincare?
options: A synthetic retinol with stronger effects
A plant-based natural alternative to retinol that offers similar anti-ageing benefits but is gentler and suitable for sensitive skin
A type of peptide for firming skin
A vitamin C derivative for brightening

question: What does niacinamide do for ageing skin specifically?
options: Only controls oil on mature skin
Improves skin texture reduces pore appearance fades dark spots and strengthens the skin barrier all of which support a more youthful look
Provides the same benefits as retinol
Only hydrates mature skin without other benefits

question: What is a serum and why is it useful in an anti-ageing routine?
options: A serum replaces moisturiser for anti-ageing
A serum is a lightweight concentrated treatment with high levels of active ingredients that penetrate deeply to target specific ageing concerns
A serum only provides hydration with no active anti-ageing ingredients
A serum is only effective when used with a face mask

question: What is the most effective non-surgical thing you can do today to prevent future skin ageing?
options: Buy the most expensive anti-ageing cream available
Apply broad spectrum sunscreen every single day without exception
Start using retinol immediately at the highest strength
Only eat anti-ageing superfoods

question: What is ceramide and how does it relate to ageing skin?
options: A type of collagen found in anti-ageing creams
A natural skin component that decreases with age weakening the skin barrier and ceramide-rich products help restore it
A strong exfoliant used in anti-ageing treatments
A type of peptide that stimulates collagen production

question: What does the term skin laxity mean?
options: When skin produces too much oil in mature skin
The loosening and sagging of skin as collagen and elastin break down with age and gravity
When skin becomes very sensitive with age
The appearance of dark spots with age

question: Which food group supports collagen production from the inside out?
options: High sugar processed foods
Vitamin C-rich foods like citrus berries and leafy greens which are essential for the body to produce collagen
Salty processed meats
Refined carbohydrates and pastries

question: Can wearing sunglasses help prevent wrinkles?
options: No sunglasses are only for eye health
Yes sunglasses reduce squinting which over time causes dynamic wrinkles around the eyes and also block UV from the delicate eye area
Only oversized sunglasses help with wrinkles
Sunglasses only prevent crow's feet not any other wrinkles

question: What is the difference between dynamic and static wrinkles?
options: Dynamic wrinkles are deeper and static wrinkles are shallower
Dynamic wrinkles appear with facial movements like smiling while static wrinkles are visible even when the face is completely relaxed
Dynamic wrinkles only appear on the forehead
Static wrinkles are caused by sun damage and dynamic ones by genetics

question: What is face yoga and does it help with ageing?
options: Face yoga is a type of facial massage that removes wrinkles permanently
Face yoga involves exercises for facial muscles which some believe helps maintain tone and lift though evidence is limited
Face yoga is a proven scientific anti-ageing treatment
Face yoga only helps if combined with injections

question: What role does vitamin A play in anti-ageing skincare?
options: Vitamin A only moisturises mature skin
Vitamin A in forms like retinol and retinoids is fundamental to cell turnover collagen stimulation and reducing fine lines and wrinkles
Vitamin A is only used in food supplements not skincare
Vitamin A only brightens dark spots without anti-ageing benefits

question: What does sleeping on a silk pillowcase potentially help with in terms of ageing?
options: It has absolutely no anti-ageing benefit
The smooth surface creates less friction on skin reducing sleep creases that can contribute to permanent wrinkle formation over time
Silk pillowcases only benefit hair not skin
Silk pillowcases make anti-ageing products absorb better

question: At what age should people consider starting a basic anti-ageing skincare routine?
options: Only after wrinkles are already very deep
Prevention can start in your mid-20s with basic SPF moisturiser and antioxidants before significant ageing signs appear
Anti-ageing skincare is only relevant after age 50
Starting before age 40 is too early for any anti-ageing products

question: What is the role of growth factors in anti-ageing skincare?
options: Growth factors exfoliate and remove dead skin
Growth factors are proteins that signal skin cells to repair and regenerate potentially stimulating collagen and improving skin texture
Growth factors only hydrate mature skin
Growth factors are only found in prescription products

question: What does skin cell turnover mean and why does it slow down with age?
options: The process where skin produces oil and it never slows down
The natural cycle where new skin cells replace old ones which slows from around 28 days in youth to 45 to 60 days in older adults causing dullness
The process of collagen production which speeds up with age
Skin cell turnover only refers to exfoliation done with skincare products

question: How does retinol help with age spots?
options: Retinol has no effect on dark spots
Retinol speeds up cell turnover which helps fade dark spots and hyperpigmentation over time as pigmented cells are shed faster
Retinol only targets wrinkles and lines
Retinol worsens dark spots by increasing sun sensitivity permanently

question: What is facial massage and what anti-ageing benefits might it offer?
options: Facial massage is only for applying skincare products more evenly
Facial massage may improve circulation reduce puffiness support lymphatic drainage and help skin look more lifted and toned over time
Facial massage removes wrinkles permanently
Facial massage is only beneficial when done by professionals

question: Why does skin around the neck age so visibly?
options: The neck produces more oil than the face
Neck skin is thin often neglected in sun protection and subject to repeated downward movement from looking at phones causing horizontal lines and sagging
Neck skin ages only in people who smoke
Neck skin ages because it is always covered by clothing

question: What is the tech neck phenomenon?
options: Using phone apps to track your skincare routine
Horizontal lines and sagging on the neck caused by repeatedly looking down at phones and screens accelerating neck ageing
A new type of skin device for anti-ageing
A term for acne caused by wearing earphones

question: What does regular gentle exfoliation do for ageing skin?
options: Exfoliation is too harsh and should be avoided on mature skin
Gentle regular exfoliation removes the buildup of dead skin cells that accumulates more with age helping skincare absorb better and revealing a brighter complexion
Exfoliation permanently reverses wrinkles
Exfoliation only benefits acne-prone skin not mature skin

question: What is the best diet approach to support slow and graceful skin ageing?
options: A high sugar high processed food diet for energy
A diet rich in antioxidants healthy fats lean proteins and vitamins that support collagen production fight free radicals and maintain skin health
Only eating collagen supplements is needed
Calorie restriction alone is the best anti-ageing diet strategy

question: Can topical collagen creams replace lost collagen in skin?
options: Yes collagen creams directly replace collagen in the dermis
No collagen molecules are too large to penetrate skin but ingredients like retinol peptides and vitamin C stimulate the body to produce its own collagen
Collagen creams work if applied for at least one year
Collagen creams work but only for people over 60

question: What is resveratrol used for in anti-ageing skincare?
options: It is a physical exfoliant for mature skin
It is a powerful plant-derived antioxidant found in grapes that helps protect skin from free radical damage and supports anti-ageing
It is a type of retinol alternative
It is a humectant that hydrates mature skin

question: What simple daily habit besides sunscreen is most recommended by dermatologists for preventing premature skin ageing?
options: Using ten or more products in your routine daily
Not smoking since smoking is one of the most significant accelerators of premature skin ageing alongside sun exposure
Only using luxury brand skincare products
Exfoliating every single day for maximum cell turnover

question: What is the most realistic expectation to have when starting an anti-ageing skincare routine?
options: Expect skin to look 20 years younger within one week
Expect gradual improvement in skin texture tone and hydration over months as skincare prevents further damage and supports natural repair
Anti-ageing products provide zero visible results
Results happen overnight with the right products
